steps.title: Identify the two independent clauses
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses: “the guide soon grew in scope and [blank]” (completed by “influence”) and “its modest French eateries were replaced with some of the most esteemed restaurants from around the world.”

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content: A comma plus a coordinating conjunction joins two independent clauses. Influence, and provides both: the comma closes the first clause and and begins the second. The other choices fail this rule—A has neither element, C lacks the conjunction, and D lacks the comma. This matches choice B — B is correct.
